standing_summary: The Stablecoins Ordinance has been in force since 1 August 2025, requiring a licence to issue fiat-referenced stablecoins in or pegged to the Hong Kong dollar. Licensees must maintain paid-up share capital of at least HK$25 million, back issued stablecoins with reserve assets at least equal to their par value at all times, and honour redemption requests at par, generally within one business day. Interested applicants were asked to submit applications by 30 September 2025 for early consideration, and on 10 April 2026 HKMA granted the first two licences -- to Anchorpoint Financial Limited and The Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation Limited. As of 28 April 2026, HKMA confirmed neither licensee had yet issued a stablecoin in the market and warned the public about fraudulent tokens falsely trading on their names.

open_items: Whether HKMA grants further licences beyond the initial two is unresolved; HKMA has described the eventual total number as likely to remain very limited.
Neither licensee has stated a specific product launch date; both have indicated a general window of mid-to-second-half 2026.
How virtual asset trading platforms may offer these stablecoins for trading is governed by a separate SFC circular (see the Exchanges (VATP) pillar).
